text:
Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) — Sample
Health Plan
Plan Overview
This is a synthetic Summary of Benefits and Coverage document created for educational purposes
only. It does not represent a real insurance plan or any real individual's coverage.
Monthly Premium
The monthly premium for this Gold PPO plan is $500. Premiums are subject to change annually based
on plan renewal terms.
Annual Deductible

on plan renewal terms.
Annual Deductible
The annual deductible for this plan is $2,000 per individual. This is the amount a member must pay out
of pocket before the plan begins covering most services.
Copay Structure
After the deductible is met, the member is responsible for a 10% copayment on most covered services.
Preventive care visits are covered at 100% with no copay required.
Out-of-Pocket Maximum

Out-of-Pocket Maximum
The out-of-pocket maximum for this plan is $6,000 per individual per plan year. Once this limit is
reached, the plan covers 100% of additional covered services for the remainder of the year.
Network Tier
This plan uses the Gold network tier, which includes access to a broad network of primary care
physicians, specialists, and hospitals.
Common Medical Events
If you have an outpatient surgery: you may pay a $250 copay per visit for a physician/surgeon and 10%

coinsurance for the facility fee. If you need mental health, behavioral health, or substance abuse
services: you may pay a $30 copay per office visit.
Prescription Drug Coverage
Generic drugs: $10 copay. Preferred brand drugs: $35 copay. Non-preferred brand drugs: $60 copay.
Specialty drugs: 20% coinsurance.

How to File a Claim — Sample Process Guide
This document is a synthetic, educational example describing a generic health insurance claims process. It does not represent any real insurance company's actual procedures.
Step 1: Receive Services
Visit an in-network or out-of-network provider for covered medical services. Be sure to bring your member ID card to each appointment.
Step 2: Provider Submits the Claim

Step 2: Provider Submits the Claim
In most cases, your healthcare provider will submit the claim directly to the insurance plan on your behalf. This typically happens within a few business days of your visit.
Step 3: Claim Review
The plan reviews the claim to verify eligibility, coverage, and medical necessity. Claims are generally processed within 10 to 30 business days, depending on complexity.
Step 4: Explanation of Benefits (EOB)

Step 4: Explanation of Benefits (EOB)
Once processed, the member receives an Explanation of Benefits (EOB) showing the billed amount, the plan's allowed amount, what the plan paid, and the member's remaining responsibility.
Step 5: Claim Status

Step 5: Claim Status
Claims are marked as Approved, Denied, or Pending. Approved claims are paid according to the plan's coverage terms. Denied claims include a reason code and instructions for filing an appeal. Pending claims may require additional documentation from the member or provider.
Step 6: Filing an Appeal

Step 6: Filing an Appeal
If a claim is denied, members have 180 days from the date of the denial notice to file a written appeal. Appeals should include supporting documentation such as medical records or a letter from the treating physician.

HEALTH PLAN ENROLLMENT FORM (SAMPLE)

This is a synthetic, educational sample form — not a real document.

Member Name: Jordan A. Sample

Date of Birth: 05/14/1988

Member ID: M1001

Selected Plan: Gold PPO (P101)

Effective Date: 01/01/2026

Employer Group: Sample Corp Inc.

Address: 123 Main Street, Springfield

Coverage Options (check one):
[ X ] Employee Only
[ ] Employee + Spouse

[ ] Employee + Family

Signature: Date:
Gold PPO: $500/month premium, $2000 deductible, 10% coinsurance, network: Gold (PPO)
Silver HMO: $300/month premium, $1500 deductible, 20% coinsurance, network: Silver (HMO)
Bronze HMO: $150/month premium, $1000 deductible, 30% coinsurance, network: Bronze (HMO)
